---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
install.packages("ggforce")     # For pie charts
install.packages("ggrepel")     # For non-overlapping labels

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(maps)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggforce)

data <- read.csv("/Users/subhashmahamkali/Downloads/tpj15853-sup-0001-files1 (1).csv")
data <- data[, c(1, 2, 5)]
colnames(data) <- c("Pinumber", "cluster", "Country")
cluster_names <- c("1" = "durra", "2" = "kafir", "3" = "caudatum", "4" = "milo/durra-bicolor", 
                   "5" = "mixed/bicolor", "6" = "guinea")
data <- data %>%
  mutate(Country = recode(Country, "S. Africa" = "South Africa", "USA" = "United States", 
                          "Prob. China" = "China", "Guatemala" = "Guatemala", 
                          "Unknown" = NA_character_, "?" = NA_character_)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Country)) %>%
  mutate(cluster = as.character(cluster),
         ClusterName = cluster_names[cluster],
         shape = "circle")

# --- 2. Load and clean dataset 2 ---
data2 <- read_excel("/Users/subhashmahamkali/Downloads/13068_2021_2016_MOESM1_ESM (1).xlsx")
colnames(data2) <- data2[1, ]; data2 <- data2[-1, ]
data2 <- data2[, c(1:2, 5:6)]
data2$Type[data2$Type == "Weedy"] <- "Wild sorghum"
data2 <- data2[data2$Type != "S.propinquum", ]
data2 <- data2[, c(2:4)]
colnames(data2) <- c("Pinumber", "Country", "cluster")
data2 <- data2 %>%
  mutate(Country = recode(Country, "S. Africa" = "South Africa", "USA" = "United States", 
                          "Prob. China" = "China", "Guatemala" = "Guatemala", 
                          "Unknown" = NA_character_, "?" = NA_character_)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Country)) %>%
  mutate(ClusterName = case_when(
    cluster == "Improved" ~ "Improved",
    cluster == "Wild sorghum" ~ "Wild sorghum",
    cluster == "Landrace" ~ "Landrace",
    TRUE ~ cluster_names[cluster]
  ),
  shape = "triangle")

# --- 3. Combine data and assign shape ---
combined_data <- bind_rows(data, data2) %>%
  mutate(shape = case_when(
    ClusterName %in% c("Improved", "Wild sorghum", "Landrace") ~ "triangle",
    TRUE ~ "circle"
  )) %>%
  filter(!is.na(ClusterName))

# --- 4. Harmonize cluster order ---
combined_data$ClusterName <- factor(combined_data$ClusterName,
  levels = c("Landrace", "Wild sorghum", "Improved", 
             "durra", "kafir", "caudatum", 
             "milo/durra-bicolor", "mixed/bicolor", "guinea"))

# --- 5. Prepare map & centroids ---
world <- map_data("world")
centroids <- world %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(long = mean(range(long)), lat = mean(range(lat)), .groups = "drop")

map_df <- combined_data %>%
  left_join(centroids, by = c("Country" = "region")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(long))

# --- 6. Cluster color palette ---
cluster_colors <- c("durra" = "green", "kafir" = "orange", "caudatum" = "purple", 
                    "milo/durra-bicolor" = "gold", "mixed/bicolor" = "brown", 
                    "guinea" = "red", "Improved" = "lightpink", 
                    "Wild sorghum" = "navy", "Landrace" = "blue")

cluster_counts <- combined_data %>%
  group_by(ClusterName) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(label = paste0(ClusterName, " (n=", n, ")"))


# --- 7. Pie Chart Data ---
pie_data <- map_df %>%
  group_by(Country, long, lat, ClusterName) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n),
         angle = 2 * pi * cumsum(prop),
         start = lag(angle, default = 0),
         end = angle,
         mid = (start + end)/2,
         r = 3)  # pie radius
pie_data <- pie_data %>%
  left_join(cluster_counts, by = "ClusterName") %>%
  mutate(ClusterLabel = label)

# --- 8. Plot with pies and labels ---
p <- ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(data = world, aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group),
               fill = "white", color = "gray70") +
  geom_arc_bar(data = pie_data,
               aes(x0 = long, y0 = lat, r0 = 0, r = r,
                   start = start, end = end, fill = ClusterName),
               alpha = 0.85, color = "black", size = 0.1) +
  #geom_text_repel(data = pie_data %>% group_by(Country) %>% slice(1),
                  #aes(x = long, y = lat, label = Country),
                  #size = 2.8, max.overlaps = 20) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cluster_colors,
                  breaks = cluster_counts$ClusterName,  
                  labels = cluster_counts$label)   +
  coord_fixed(1.3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "",
       x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude", fill = "Cluster") +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
        #plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
        )

# --- 9. Save final outputs ---
ggsave("/Users/subhashmahamkali/Documents/gwas_sap/graphs/1.phenotype_distribution/geo_dist_with_pies.pdf",
       plot = p, device = "pdf", width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 500, useDingbats = FALSE)

```
